senses.definition: any of the hard pieces making up the skeleton in humans and some animals

examples.x: She twisted her ankle and felt a shooting pain in the bone.
The doctor confirmed that the broken bone would heal with time.
The surgeon performed a bone graft to repair the damaged bone.

senses.definition: a place in the body where two bones meet, enabling one of them to bend or move around

examples.x: The knee is a complex joint that allows for bending and straightening of the leg.
He underwent surgery to repair a damaged joint in his thumb, restoring functionality and relieving pain.

senses.definition: an elastic tissue that supports or connects joints in an infant and turns into skeleton during growth

examples.x: Cartilage lacks blood vessels, which affects its ability to heal.
The nose and ears are supported by cartilage rather than bone.
Cartilage cushions joints and prevents bones from rubbing against each other.
Osteoarthritis is a condition where cartilage in joints deteriorates over time.
Chondrocytes are cells responsible for producing and maintaining cartilage.

senses.definition: a transformed cartilaginous structure that has undergone ossification
(anatomy) a strong band of tissue which connects two bones or cartilages or keeps organs in place
a fluid-filled sac that reduces friction between tissues such as bones, tendons, and muscles
the central part of the skeleton that includes the skull, vertebral column, and ribcage
a single muscle that is located along the central axis of the body, including the head, neck, and trunk
a tendon in the leg that attaches the muscles of the calf to the bone of the heel

examples.x: The Achilles tendon connects the calf muscles to the heel bone, playing a crucial role in walking, running, and jumping.
A sudden increase in physical activity can strain the Achilles tendon, leading to a condition known as Achilles tendinitis.
Athletes, particularly runners and basketball players, are at higher risk of Achilles tendon injuries due to the repetitive stress placed on this tendon.
A rupture of the Achilles tendon is a serious injury that often requires surgical intervention and extensive rehabilitation to regain full function.
Stretching and strengthening exercises can help prevent injuries to the Achilles tendon by improving flexibility and resilience.

senses.definition: (anatomy) an elastic cord or band that connects a muscle to a bone

examples.x: Tendons transmit the force generated by muscles to bones, enabling movement.
Physical therapy can help strengthen tendons and improve flexibility.
Tendons are composed primarily of collagen fibers.

senses.definition: the bony structure that surrounds and provides protection for a person's or animal's brain

examples.x: In anatomy class, they learned about the different bones that form the human skull.
The forensic investigator examined the skull to determine the cause of death.
The human skull has several openings, including the eye sockets and nasal cavity.
The museum displayed a collection of animal skulls from various species.
The skull protects the brain, one of the most vital organs in the body.

senses.definition: a piece of body tissue that is made tight or relaxed when we want to move a particular part of our body

examples.x: He massaged his sore muscles to ease the tension.
Regular exercise can help build and tone muscles.
She felt sore in her leg muscles after a long hike.
The physical therapist recommended exercises to strengthen weak muscles.
The weightlifter's strong muscles helped him lift heavy weights.

senses.definition: (anatomy) one of the five tendons at the back of someone's knee
(anatomy) a muscle that moves a part of the body away from its normal position by contraction
a line of connected bones going down from your neck to tail bone in the middle of the back

examples.x: Good posture helps support the backbone and reduces strain on the back.
Regular exercise can help strengthen the muscles around the backbone.
The doctor explained that the backbone consists of individual vertebrae.
The gymnast demonstrated incredible flexibility in her backbone during her routine.
The yoga instructor emphasized the importance of stretching the backbone in class.

senses.definition: the large muscle at the front of the upper part of the arm, which flexes the forearm

examples.x: He flexed his biceps to show off his strength.
Weightlifting exercises can build and tone the biceps.
Tendons connect the biceps muscle to the bones of the forearm.
Bicep curls target the biceps and help improve arm strength.
Swelling and pain in the biceps can result from overuse or injury.

senses.definition: a long flat bone at the center of the chest that connects the ribs and the shoulder girdle
the bone that is just below the eye

examples.x: The makeup artist applied highlighter to accentuate the model's cheekbones, creating a sculpted look.
The boxer's opponent landed a powerful punch that fractured his cheekbone, forcing him to withdraw from the match.
In some cultures, prominent cheekbones are considered a symbol of beauty and elegance.
She admired her grandmother's high cheekbones, a trait that seemed to run in the family.

senses.definition: (anatomy) the structure that encloses the inner surface of the eye which consists of the muscle which makes the eye move
(anatomy) a bone of the shoulder girdle that connects the breastbone to the shoulder bone
either of the pair of bones that go across the top of the chest from the base of the neck to the shoulders

examples.x: He fractured his collarbone during the football game and needed to wear a sling for several weeks.
The x-ray revealed a clean break in the collarbone, requiring immediate medical attention.
She wore a strapless dress that elegantly showcased her collarbone and shoulders.
The doctor palpated the area around his collarbone to check for any signs of injury.
The athlete's strong collarbone structure helped support the weight of heavy lifting.

senses.definition: (anatomy) the bone structure that surrounds and protects the brain

examples.x: The cranium protects the delicate structures of the brain.
Skull fractures can occur if there is a severe impact to the cranium.
A CT scan revealed a small abnormality in the base of her cranium.
Helmets are designed to protect the cranium during sports and activities.
The cranium is essential for maintaining the shape and structure of the head.

senses.definition: (anatomy) a large muscle, triangular in shape that covers the shoulder joint and helps the arm to abduct and rotate
a muscle that helps a body part or limb be stretched out by contraction

examples.x: A strain in the extensor tendons of his hand resulted in limited movement and discomfort.
The extensor reflex is an involuntary response that causes the leg to kick out when the knee is tapped.

senses.definition: (anatomy) the longest bone in the top part of the human leg between the hip and the knee

examples.x: The femur is the longest and strongest bone in the human body, extending from the hip to the knee.
A fracture of the femur can result from severe trauma, such as a car accident or a significant fall, and often requires surgical repair.
The head of the femur fits into the hip socket, forming the hip joint, which allows for a wide range of leg movements.
Osteoporosis can weaken the femur, making it more susceptible to fractures, especially in older adults.
The femur plays a crucial role in supporting the weight of the body and enabling activities such as walking, running, and jumping.

senses.definition: (anatomy) a long bone in the arm that extends from the shoulder to the elbow
(anatomy) the outer and narrower of the two bones between the ankle and the knee
(anatomy) a muscle that allows one to bend a limb or body part by contraction
either of the bones that form the jaw, particularly the lower jaw

examples.x: The boxer's opponent landed a powerful punch to his jawbone, causing him to stagger backward.
The dentist examined the x-ray images to assess the condition of the patient's jawbone before proceeding with the dental implant surgery.
She placed her hand on her swollen jawbone, feeling the tenderness caused by the infected tooth.
The forensic anthropologist studied the shape and size of the jawbone to determine the individual's age and gender.
The chewing motion of the jawbone allows for the grinding and breaking down of food during digestion.

senses.definition: a small bone that covers the front of the knee joint
(anatomy) (zoology) either half of the jawbone, especially the lower jawbone that helps a vertebrate open and close its mouth
(anatomy) any of the five bones of the foot between the ankle and the toes
(biology) the overall arrangement or the system of muscles in a human or an animal's body
(anatomy) the trapezoidal bone that is at the base and lower back of the cranium, though which the spinal cord passes
the singular bone located at the front of the skull, forming the forehead and upper eye sockets for structural support and protection
a cranial bone that forms the sides and roof of the skull, offering protection for the brain
a butterfly-shaped bone, contributing to its structure and housing important structures
a cranial bone located on the sides and base of the skull, housing the ear structures and contributing to jaw movement
a facial bone that forms the prominence of the cheek and connects various other facial bones
a facial bone that contributes to the structure of the cheek and forms part of the eye socket
a facial bone that forms the central part of the face, supporting the upper teeth
(anatomy) a small bone triangular in shape that covers and protects the front part of the knee joint
(anatomy) the large round bone structure that the limbs and the spine are joined to, which also protects the abdominal organs

examples.x: The pelvis is a bony structure located at the base of the spine, consisting of the hip bones, sacrum, and coccyx.
The pelvis is a key component of the human skeletal system, providing support and protection to internal organs.

senses.definition: (anatomy) any of the four large muscles at the front of the thigh that allow the knee joint to stretch
a muscle at the front of the thigh that helps the knee joint stretch
(anatomy) the larger and thicker bone of the human forearm extending from the elbow to the thumb side of the wrist
each of the curved bones surrounding the chest to protect the organs inside

examples.x: The doctor examined the patient's ribs after the fall to check for any fractures.
She felt a sharp pain in her ribs after lifting a heavy object.
The ribs expand and contract with each breath, helping to facilitate respiration.
He broke a rib while skiing down the slope and had to be airlifted to the hospital.
The boxer wore protective padding around his ribs to minimize the risk of injury during the match.

senses.definition: (anatomy) the bony structure in the chest formed by the ribs which protects organs in the thoracic cavity
(anatomy) a large bone at the lower part of the spinal column between the two hip bones of the pelvis
the inner and larger bone of the pair of bones between the ankle and the knee
(anatomy) each of the pair of triangular bones that connect the humerus to the clavicle

examples.x: The scapula, commonly known as the shoulder blade, is a flat, triangular bone located in the upper back that connects the humerus (upper arm bone) with the clavicle (collarbone).
The muscles surrounding the scapula, such as the rotator cuff muscles, help facilitate a wide range of shoulder movements, including lifting, rotating, and stabilizing the arm.
An injury to the scapula, such as a fracture or dislocation, can result in significant pain and limited mobility of the shoulder.
Physical therapy exercises aimed at strengthening the muscles around the scapula can help improve posture and prevent shoulder injuries.
The scapula has several important bony landmarks, including the acromion and the coracoid process, which serve as attachment points for muscles and ligaments.

senses.definition: each of the pair of triangular bones that connect the upper arm bone with the collar bone
an elastic cord or band that connects a muscle to a bone

examples.x: In anatomy class, we learned that a sinew is made up of tough, fibrous tissue.
The injury to the athlete’s leg was actually a torn sinew, which required surgery to repair.

senses.definition: the structure of bones supporting the body of an animal or a person

examples.x: The X-ray revealed a fracture in his skeleton.
Archaeologists carefully examined the ancient skeleton.
The skeleton of a whale is much larger than that of a human.
The doctor showed an image of the skeleton during the lecture.
Scientists discovered a dinosaur skeleton in the desert.

senses.definition: the row of small bones that are joined together down the center of the back of the body

examples.x: The surgeon carefully examined the patient's spine to assess the extent of the injury.
Proper posture helps maintain the natural alignment of the spine and prevents back pain.
The x-ray revealed a curvature in the spine, indicating a case of scoliosis.
Strengthening the muscles around the spine can provide better support and reduce strain.
In yoga, many poses are designed to improve flexibility and strength in the spine.

senses.definition: (anatomy) a ring of muscle that can open or close an opening, such as anus, in the body
(anatomy) a long flat bone at the center of the chest that connects the ribs to the shoulder girdle
the small triangular bone at the end of the spinal column in humans or tailless primates
(anatomy) any of the cluster of seven small bones in the foot between the ankle and upper foot
the longest bone in the human skeleton which is between the hip and the knee
(anatomy) the inner and larger of the two bones between the ankle and the knee
(anatomy) the three-headed muscle of the arm that functions as the extensor of the elbow joint
(anatomy) the thinner and longer bone of the human forearm extending from the elbow to the wrist
(anatomy) any of the bony segments and cartilages of the spinal column

examples.x: Injuries to the vertebra can result in paralysis or loss of sensation.
The vertebra are stacked on top of each other, separated by intervertebral discs.
The human spine consists of 33 vertebra, including the coccyx and sacrum.

senses.definition: a small bone found in the middle ear that helps transmit sound vibrations and facilitate hearing
an opening or a hole, particularly in a bone structure
the lower bone of the face containing the chin and the bottom teeth

examples.x: He clenched his jaw in frustration when he heard the news.
The dentist checked the alignment of her jaw to ensure there were no issues with her bite.
The boxer suffered a broken jaw after the intense match.
She had to undergo surgery to correct a misaligned jaw.
Chewing gum for too long can sometimes cause soreness in the jaw.
